question_text: Which of the following best describes what happens when sound travels from air into water?

options.label: A
options.text: The frequency increases and the wavelength increases

options.label: B
options.text: The frequency stays the same but the wavelength increases

options.label: C
options.text: The frequency stays the same but the wavelength decreases

options.label: D
options.text: Both the frequency and wavelength decrease

correct_answer: The frequency stays the same but the wavelength decreases
explanation: When sound travels from one medium to another, the frequency (determined by the vibrating source) remains constant. However, sound travels faster in water than in air. Using the wave equation v = f × λ, if velocity increases and frequency stays the same, the wavelength must decrease to maintain the relationship.
